name: Blue Mesa Corncakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and place an ovenproof dish filled with about 2 inches of water on a rack set in the lower third of the oven. Place the top rack in the upper third of the oven.
2. Grease a 13x9-inch baking pan and set aside.
3. Cream shortening and margarine in a medium bowl using a mixer's medium speed. Change to high speed and whip the fats until fluffy, about 15 minutes.
4. At low speed, add masa harina and mix for about 2 minutes or until well combined. Add cold water and mix at medium speed for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl to keep it blended.
5. In batches, puree corn in a blender or food processor until smooth. If blades clog, add a bit of water or milk. Reserve the processed corn.
6. In a large mixing bowl, combine cornmeal, sugar, whipping cream, baking powder, and salt.
7. To the cornmeal mixture, add the creamed fats and reserved processed corn. Mix until the batter is well blended.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top evenly.
9. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
